input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

## 1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime

`StaticResource` resolves **once** at XAML load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when you swap the merged dictionary:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates when dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes in code like:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme());   // or LightTheme
```

## 2. Android restarts on dark mode toggle

Android recreates the `Activity` when `uiMode` changes. Tell it not to by adding `ConfigChanges.UiMode` in your **MainActivity.cs**:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode  // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

To also **react** to the OS toggle, override `OnConfigurationChanged`:

```csharp
public override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);

    var isDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
    mergedDicts.Clear();
    mergedDicts.Add(isDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
}
```

**TL;DR** — `DynamicResource` everywhere for theme colors, and `ConfigChanges.UiMode` on your `MainActivity` to prevent the restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and doesn't update when the resource dictionary changes. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead.

2. **App restart on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user toggles dark mode in Android settings, the app restarts. This is because Android recreates the Activity by default when the UI mode (dark/light) changes. They need to handle the `uiMode` configuration change in their Android manifest or activity attributes.


I'll provide a straightforward solution addressing both the binding approach and the activity recreation issue.
